hello paint
brand book · v1 · custom paint-by-number kits from your photo

The idea

A photo goes in, a paintable kit comes out. The brand should feel like a warm friend who hands you the fun part.

hello paint is cozy and cheery, friendly to all ages, and made by a person. The look is flat and hand-painted, never techy, never an arcade. The palette leads with warm cream and a raspberry "hello," with teal and sunshine doing the eye-catching, and it stays clear of orange on purpose so the brand reads as human-made.

Logo family

Three marks, one wordmark, one palette. Use each by role.

Sticker & stamp

Waving hand

A painted hand waving hello, with a wink. The friendly extra.

Main logo

Before & after

Half photo, half numbered kit. The mark that explains the product. Supporting art (not the logo).

Profile avatar

Hello bubble

A hello bubble full of paint. Your round photo everywhere. Primary logo.

Lockup, reversed, and avatar

Reversed on ink, and the bubble in a circle. Full set in 01-logos and 03-avatars.

Clear space & minimum size

Keep clear space around the mark equal to the height of the lowercase "h" in the wordmark. Never crowd it.

Minimum sizes: icon and favicon hold at 16 px; the full lockup stays at least 120 px wide on screen and 25 mm in print.

All marks are flat vector, render in one color, and survive a circle crop. Use the one-color stamp for stickers and kit labels.

Color

Raspberry#E64C81
Teal#15A39A
Sunshine#F6C744
Leaf#5BA86B
Sky#BFE6F2
Plum ink#312B3D
Cream#FBF4E8
Off-white#FFFDF8
Blush#FBD0DD

For colored text on a light background, use the deeper variants so it stays legible: deep berry #B83A66 and deep teal #0F756D. Body text is plum ink on cream. Bright raspberry and teal are for headlines, shapes, and the logo, not paragraphs. The palette never uses orange.

Text on backgroundContrastUse
Plum ink on cream12.44Body text
Cream on plum ink12.44Reversed body
Deep teal on cream5.07Links, teal text
Deep berry on cream5.01Berry text
Raspberry on cream3.36Headlines only

Typography

Fraunces, headings

Optical serif, weight 600. Warm and editorial. One headline per view.

Nunito Sans, body and wordmark

Friendly sans. Weights 400 and 700 for text, 800 for the wordmark and kit numbers.

RoleSize (px)
Display, h140
Section, h228
Subhead, h322
Body17
Caption14

The wordmark stays lowercase with a single space. Sentence case everywhere, never Title Case or all caps.

Voice & tone

Cozy cool girl. Plain over clever. Invite, do not instruct.

Talk the way you would hype a friend. Lead with the before and after. Ask for the worst pet photo. Keep the handle lowercase. Avoid anything that sounds techy, coded, or arcade.

hi! send a photo, get a paint-by-number kit your photo, painted by you turn your photo into a painting you can make no drawing needed, ever

Applications

Etsy shop banner

Instagram post

Pinterest pin

Etsy listing tile

Do & don't

Do

  • Keep "hello" raspberry and "paint" ink.
  • Lead layouts with cream so the brights pop.
  • Use the one-color stamp for stickers and labels.
  • Pair the snapshot logo with your before and after photo.
  • Give the mark its clear space.

Don't

  • Add orange or terracotta anywhere.
  • Capitalize or close up the wordmark.
  • Add gradients, shadows, or pixel and arcade styling.
  • Set body text in bright raspberry or teal.
  • Stretch, rotate, or recolor the marks.

hello paint brand book v1 · prepared May 30, 2026 · marks are original vector art · type is Fraunces and Nunito Sans (free Google Fonts) · palette avoids orange on purpose.
